summary: Hide the desktop-only OS-trash action from browser workspaces instead of permanently deleting IndexedDB records behind misleading trash copy.

decisions.decision: Make OS trash an explicit platform capability, omit the action by default, and enable it only for the desktop composition root that supplies a real trash implementation.
decisions.rationale: The browser adapter's IndexedDB trash method permanently removed a record, so presenting it as an OS-trash operation violated FR-13.3 and gave users no recovery path. Capability-gated absence is the required web behavior.

testEvidence.criterion: Browser/default project panels expose no trash action, while an explicitly capable desktop panel retains the functional action.
testEvidence.test: Render the project panel without a trash capability and assert the named control is absent, then rerender with the capability and assert it is visible; retain the existing functional desktop trash journey.

red.command: Independent C13 adversarial source audit of ProjectPanel and indexeddb-project-storage
red.outcome: RED: the shared panel always rendered Move to trash while the browser storage adapter permanently deleted the IndexedDB record, producing one critical honesty defect.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/files/ProjectPanel.test.tsx tests/ui/files/FilesActivity.test.tsx; pnpm.cmd typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: both focused files passed 7/7 tests in 1.46 s and strict TypeScript exited 0. The regression covers absent-by-default and explicitly enabled trash states.

testEvidence.criterion: Real browser journeys assert that OS trash is absent while moved IndexedDB files remain durable across reload.
testEvidence.test: Run the complete single-worker Playwright browser acceptance gate after replacing stale trash-action expectations with absence and retained-file assertions.

red.outcome: RED: 20/22 journeys passed; two timed out waiting for the intentionally removed Move to trash controls.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 22 browser journeys passed in 38.5 s. The real IndexedDB journey proves the moved source remains byte-for-byte durable after reload while no OS-trash control is exposed.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
